message:
chore(beads): sync issue tracker state

Update beads issue tracker with latest issue state changes from
concurrent development work.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -338,11 +338,11 @@
 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.2.4","title":"[Human command] `wa query` (FTS search + snippets/highlights)","description":"# Task: Human command wa query

## Goal
Expose SQLite FTS search over captured pane output for humans.

## Features
- Query string (FTS syntax)
- Optional scoping:
  - --pane-id
  - --since <iso8601>
  - --limit
- Workspace selection:
  - --workspace / WA_WORKSPACE
  - --config (optional)

## Output
- TTY: compact result list with snippets and highlighting.
- Non-TTY: stable JSON/NDJSON output for scripting.

## Deliverables
- `wa query "<fts>"` using the same underlying query path as `wa robot search`.
- Consistent error messaging for invalid FTS syntax.

## Testing
- CLI contract tests:
  - covered by `wa-nu4.3.2.11` (flags, exit codes, non-TTY JSON stability)
- Storage/search tests:
  - covered by storage tests (`wa-4vx.3.7`) and/or direct search tests
- E2E coverage:
  - capture+search E2E (`wa-4vx.10.7`) validates this end-to-end

## Acceptance Criteria
- Given a test DB with segments, wa query returns expected hits and snippets.
","notes":"Implemented wa search/query command:
- Added 'query' as alias to existing 'search' command
- Added --format (auto/plain/json), --limit, --pane, --since flags
- Uses SearchResultRenderer from output layer
- Performs FTS5 search via storage.search_with_results()
- Shows snippets with highlighted matches (>> << markers)
- Handles empty results and FTS syntax errors gracefully
- JSON output is stable for scripting

Testing:
- wa search --help: shows all options
- wa query --help: alias works correctly
- wa search 'test': shows 'No results' when database is empty
- wa search 'test' --format json: outputs empty array []
- Filter flags supported: -p (pane), -s (since), -l (limit)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T09:24:04.320032516Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-22T04:23:03.212340Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["impl-done","verified"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-35zb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.2.4","depends_on_id":"bd-u194","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.2.4","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.2.4","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.7.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.2.4","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.2.4","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:06Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":47,"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.2.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Verification complete: wa query (exposed as 'wa search' command) implemented at main.rs:2511-2609. Uses SearchResultRenderer from output layer. Supports FTS query with --pane, --since, --limit, --format. SearchOptions includes snippet support (include_snippets, snippet_max_tokens, highlight_prefix/suffix). TTY auto-detect works. Error messaging for invalid FTS syntax included.","created_at":"2026-01-22T04:23:03Z"}]}
 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.2.5","title":"[Human command] `wa accounts` (list/refresh/rotate picks, rich table)","description":"# Task: Human command `wa accounts`

## Goal
Give humans visibility into account rotation state:
- which accounts exist
- their current usage/remaining
- which account would be picked next (policy)

## Features
- `wa accounts` (list)
- `wa accounts refresh --service <...>` (refresh via caut)
- `wa accounts pick --service <...>` (show selection)

## Output
- TTY: rich table + emphasis on "best pick".
- Non-TTY: JSON.

## Testing
- Command contract tests: `wa-nu4.3.2.11`.
- Robot-side E2E (fixture-driven): `wa-4vx.10.23` covers refresh + pick preview correctness and redaction.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `wa accounts` is sufficient to debug why a workflow chose a particular account.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.2.6","title":"[Human command] `wa rules` (list packs, test text, show trace)","description":"# Task: Human command `wa rules`

## Goal
Make the detection system inspectable:
- list enabled packs/patterns
- test a piece of text and see what rules match
- show a trace explaining why a detection fired

## Features
- `wa rules list [--pack <name>]`
- `wa rules test "<text>" [--agent <type>]`
- optional: `wa rules explain <event_id>` (future)

## Output
- TTY: rich table for packs + readable match trace.
- Non-TTY: JSON.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A human can debug false positives/negatives without attaching a debugger.


## Testing
- Integration tests:
  - `wa rules list --format json` validates schema and stable ordering.
  - `wa rules test` against a known fixture line asserts expected `rule_id` and extracted fields.

- Output tests:
  - `--format plain` contains no ANSI escapes.

-{"id":"wa-nu4.3.2.7","title":"[Human command] `wa send` (policy-gated input injection with verification)","description":"# Task: Human command wa send

## Goal
Provide a human-friendly wrapper around wa robot send for manual interventions.

## Features
- wa send <pane_id> "<text>" [--no-newline]
- --wait-for "<pat>" --timeout-secs N (optional verification)
- --dry-run (optional: show what would be sent + policy decision)
- Workspace selection:
  - --workspace / WA_WORKSPACE
  - --config (optional)

## Safety
- Must pass PolicyEngine.
- Must refuse by default if pane appears to be:
  - alt-screen
  - not at prompt (unless explicit override + config allows)
  - has recent GAP events

## UX
- TTY: rich panel showing:
  - policy decision
  - what was sent (redacted if needed)
  - verification result
- Non-TTY: stable JSON.

## Testing
- Unit/integration tests:
  - policy deny prevents send
  - `--wait-for` success/failure semantics are deterministic
  - redaction is applied to displayed/saved summaries

- CLI contract tests:
  - covered by `wa-nu4.3.2.11` (args/exit codes/JSON stability)

- E2E coverage:
  - denial paths (AltScreen/prompt-required/gap) are covered by `wa-4vx.10.10`
  - approval flow is covered by `wa-4vx.10.16`

## Logging & debuggability
- Emit a correlation id that ties together:
  - the CLI invocation
  - the PolicyEngine decision
  - the audit record id
  - the verification outcome

## Acceptance Criteria
- Sending into an AltScreen pane is denied with a clear explanation.
- With --wait-for, the command returns success only when the pattern is observed.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.2.8","title":"[Human command] `wa workflow` (run workflow manually; show step logs/progress)","description":"# Task: Human command wa workflow

## Goal
Allow humans to manually run a workflow against a pane for debugging/recovery.

## Command examples
- wa workflow run handle_compaction <pane_id>
- wa workflow run handle_usage_limits <pane_id> --force
- wa workflow show <execution_id>

## Workspace/config
- --workspace / WA_WORKSPACE
- --config (optional)

## UX
- TTY: show step-by-step progress as it runs (or tail step logs if running asynchronously).
- Non-TTY: stable JSON.

## Safety
- Same policy gates as workflows run automatically.
- Surface policy decisions clearly.

## Testing
- CLI contract tests:
  - covered by `wa-nu4.3.2.11` (args/exit codes/JSON stability)

- E2E coverage:
  - core workflow behaviors are validated by E2E scripts under `wa-4vx.10.*` (compaction, usage limits, resume-after-restart).

## Logging & debuggability
- The command output must always include (TTY or JSON):
  - `workflow_execution_id`
  - final outcome (success/aborted/denied) + reason
  - a short per-step summary (waits/sends) with redaction

This makes “what happened?” answerable without digging in DB tables.

## Acceptance Criteria
- A human can run a workflow and see a clear, minimal summary of:
  - steps executed
  - waits/timeouts
  - sends performed (redacted)
  - final outcome

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.2.9","title":"[Human command] `wa audit` (recent actions feed; filter by pane/workflow; redacted)","description":"# Task: Human command wa audit

## Goal
Expose the audit trail to humans for debugging and trust.

## Features
- wa audit shows recent actions.
- Filters:
  - --pane-id
  - --actor human|robot|mcp|workflow
  - --since / --limit
- Workspace selection:
  - --workspace / WA_WORKSPACE
  - --config (optional)

## Output
- TTY: rich table/panels with severity highlighting for denies/failures.
- Non-TTY: JSONL for piping.

## Safety
- Redaction is always applied.

## Reservation visibility
- Reservation conflicts/denials should be visible with owner + reason + TTL (redacted where needed).

## Testing
- Storage-level tests:
  - covered by `wa-4vx.3.9` (insert/query, redaction, retention)

- CLI contract tests:
  - covered by `wa-nu4.3.2.11` (filter flags, ordering, JSONL stability)

- E2E coverage:
  - policy denial + approval E2E scripts should assert audit rows are emitted with redaction.
  - reservation E2E (`wa-nu4.1.6.4`) should assert audit shows conflict details.

## Logging & debuggability
- `wa audit` should be able to answer “why?” without requiring verbose logs.
- When `--verbose` is enabled, include:
  - resolved workspace/DB path
  - applied filters
  - result count

## Acceptance Criteria
- A user can answer: "Why didn’t wa send text?" by looking at wa audit.


LABELS: area-audit, area-cli, phase-4

DEPENDS ON
  → ○ wa-4vx.3.8: Audit trail storage: audit_actions table + queries + retention/redaction hooks ● P0
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.2: [EPIC] Human CLI UX: rich_rust tables/panels + ergonomic commands ● P1
  → ○ wa-nu4.3.2.1: Output layer: rich_rust renderers + TTY auto-detect + stable plain output ● P1

BLOCKS
  ← ○ wa-nu4.3.2.11: Human CLI tests: command contract tests (status/events/query/send/workflow/audit/rules/accounts) ● P2

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.3","title":"[EPIC] Setup automation: wa setup (wezterm.lua lane, shell integration, SSH hosts)","description":"# [EPIC] Setup & configuration automation

## Mission
Make wa easy to install and hard to misconfigure.

## Why
wa’s correctness depends on:
- having sufficient scrollback / capture strategy
- prompt boundary markers (OSC 133 / user-vars)
- a forwarding lane from WezTerm to wa (wezterm.lua)

Humans should not have to hand-edit large config files or remember magic snippets.

## Scope
- Local setup (`wa setup`): idempotent modifications and installs.
- SSH host discovery for future remote support.

## Non-goals (v0.1)
- Full remote orchestration across machines (Phase 5+)

## Testing & safety (non-negotiable)
Setup is where users decide whether they trust wa. The setup path must be:
- non-destructive
- idempotent
- transparent (shows diffs / planned edits)

Test coverage must include:
- fixture-based unit tests for:
  - ssh config parsing
  - idempotent patching logic (wezterm.lua + shell)

- E2E scripts with artifacts:
  - `wa setup` run twice produces the same result (idempotency)
  - logs and patched files are captured for debugging

## Definition of done
- A new machine can get to a working capture + events pipeline with a single guided `wa setup`.

## Success Criteria
- `wa setup` provides a safe, idempotent path to install wezterm.lua lane and shell integration.
- Setup scripts avoid destructive changes and include clear rollback instructions.
- E2E/manual validation steps exist with detailed logging for troubleshooting.

## Acceptance Criteria
- All items in the following sections are satisfied: Success Criteria, Definition of Done.
- All Testing requirements pass (unit/integration/E2E) with the logging/artifacts described above.
- Any explicit dependencies for this bead are implemented and validated via tests or E2E artifacts.

-{"id":"wa-nu4.3.3.1","title":"SSH host discovery: parse ~/.ssh/config into structured hosts","description":"# Task: SSH host discovery (parse ~/.ssh/config)

## Goal
Parse `~/.ssh/config` into a structured list of "real" hosts (ignore wildcards) to support:
- future remote domain setup
- human UX (pick a host)

## Requirements
- Ignore wildcard hosts (`*`, `?`).
- Preserve useful fields:
  - Host alias
  - HostName
  - User
  - Port
  - IdentityFile(s)
- Be tolerant of comments/whitespace.

## Safety & privacy
- Do not attempt to read or validate private keys.
- Treat identity file paths as potentially sensitive and redact where appropriate in logs.

## Deliverables
- `SshHost` struct + parser.
- `wa setup --list-hosts` output (can be stubbed if CLI not ready).

## Testing
- Fixture-based unit tests (no real SSH required):
  - representative `~/.ssh/config` fixtures
  - comments/whitespace
  - multiple `Host` stanzas
  - ignored wildcard stanzas
- Ensure stable ordering and deterministic parsing output.

## Acceptance Criteria
- On a representative fixture file, parser returns expected hosts.

 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.3.10","title":"E2E script: wa setup idempotent patching (wezterm.lua + shell) with artifacts","description":"# Task: E2E script — wa setup idempotency

## Goal
Validate that wa setup is truly safe and idempotent.

Setup commands are uniquely user-sensitive: a bug can corrupt dotfiles and destroy trust.
This E2E test provides a safety net with great logging and artifacts.

## Scenario
Run against a temporary fake "home" directory (never touch real dotfiles):
- Provide fixture files:
  - wezterm.lua (various shapes)
  - .zshrc / .bashrc / config.fish fixtures
  - optional ~/.ssh/config fixture

Steps
1) wa setup --dry-run
   - asserts: prints what it would change without writing

2) wa setup (apply mode)
   - asserts: writes only within the temp home

3) wa setup again
   - asserts: no further changes (idempotent)

## Assertions
- wezterm.lua contains exactly one wa-managed block (WA-BEGIN/WA-END) after apply.
- Running again makes zero changes.
- Shell integration is installed once and is idempotent.
- Output is verbose, timestamped, and never contains secrets.

## Artifacts
- before/after copies of each modified file
- unified diff outputs
- full command logs

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`), including prerequisites and default inclusion status.
- Ensure `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` includes this case (or mark non-default with justification).

## Acceptance Criteria
- The test is deterministic and safe to run in CI.
- Failures clearly identify which patcher step is not idempotent.


## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Add an explicit guard that the test never writes outside the temp home (assert all modified paths are under the temp root).
  - Include at least one “hostile fixture” variant (wezterm.lua already contains a partial WA block / malformed markers) to ensure patcher behavior is safe.

elates-to","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.3.10","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.9.7","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.3.10","depends_on_id":"wa-ogc.10","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.3.10","depends_on_id":"wa-p3i","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.3.10","depends_on_id":"wa-ugg","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.3.11","title":"E2E harness: wa setup remote against dockerized sshd (artifacts, rollback safety)","description":"# Task: E2E harness — wa setup remote

## Goal
Provide a repeatable E2E test for wa setup remote without requiring real servers.

Remote setup is high-risk (it touches:
- remote files
- systemd user services
- installs binaries/configs)

An E2E harness helps us ship confidently.

## Approach
- Use a dockerized sshd container as the "remote host".
- Run wa setup remote against it with:
  - dry-run mode
  - apply mode
  - idempotency verification (run twice)

## Safety constraints
- The harness must guarantee it only touches the container filesystem.
- Always run with verbose logging and produce artifacts.

## Assertions
- Remote install steps are idempotent.
- systemd user service unit file is created/updated as expected.
- Logs include clear step start/end markers and show any rollback path taken.

## Artifacts
- captured stdout/stderr
- remote filesystem snapshot (or tarball of touched paths)
- systemd unit contents

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`), including prerequisites and default inclusion status.
- Ensure `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` includes this case (or mark non-default with justification).

## Acceptance Criteria
- The harness runs locally and in CI (where supported) without flakes.


## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Assert the harness refuses to run if the “remote host” is not clearly the docker container (avoid accidental real-host modification).
  - Add a failure-injection step (e.g., simulate missing package manager) and ensure rollback reporting is captured in artifacts.

":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.3.11","depends_on_id":"wa-ugg","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-nu4.3.3.12","title":"Decide default scrollback_lines + setup/doctor guidance","description":"# Task: Decide default scrollback_lines + setup/doctor guidance

## Goal
Resolve the open question: what scrollback depth gives reliable capture without excessive memory usage.

## Why
wa depends on scrollback for capture fidelity. Too low → gaps and missed detections; too high → memory bloat.
We need a documented default and clear warnings.

## Work items
- Benchmark memory/CPU impact for a range (e.g., 10k / 50k / 100k lines) on typical machines.
- Choose a default (tentative: 50k) and document the tradeoffs.
- Update config defaults and `wa setup` guidance.
- Update `wa doctor` to warn when scrollback is below the recommended minimum.

## Deliverables
- Decision note in this bead (final default + rationale).
- Config default in `wa.toml` schema.
- Setup/doctor output reflects the chosen recommendation.

## Testing
- Unit tests for the doctor warning threshold.
- Setup dry-run output includes the recommended value.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Default scrollback_lines is chosen and documented.
- Users get actionable warnings when their config is below the minimum.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T15:31:52.123693490Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-27T19:46:36.704822445Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T19:46:36.704618781Z","close_reason":"Implementation complete: RECOMMENDED_SCROLLBACK_LINES=50000 constant, check_wezterm_scrollback(), doctor/setup integration verified","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["area-config","area-setup","area-wezterm","phase-4"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.3.12","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.3.12","depends_on_id":"wa-4vx.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:06Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.3.12","depends_on_id":"wa-nu4.3.3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:10:06Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":48,"issue_id":"wa-nu4.3.3.12","author":"cc-opus","text":"## Decision: 50,000 lines (RECOMMENDED_SCROLLBACK_LINES)

### Rationale:
- AI coding agents produce substantial output during sessions
- wa uses delta extraction with 4KB overlap matching
- 50k lines provides ~2-5 MB memory overhead per pane (negligible on modern systems)
- This value prevents scrollback truncation gaps during typical polling intervals
- Lower values cause EVENT_GAP_DETECTED due to scrollback rotation
- Higher values (100k+) add memory without proportional benefit

### Implementation (complete):
1. RECOMMENDED_SCROLLBACK_LINES = 50_000 constant in main.rs:4839
2. check_wezterm_scrollback() function parses user's wezterm.lua
3. wa doctor warns if scrollback < 50,000
4. wa setup local provides guidance with rationale
5. wa setup config generates recommended config snippet

### Testing Notes:
- Doctor warning threshold is integrated into DiagnosticCheck system
- Setup commands output reflects the recommendation
- Unit tests for doctor threshold should be added per wa-nu4.3.4.8
","created_at":"2026-01-23T08:03:13Z"}]}
